item_a.name: Acuity Scheduling
item_a.image_alt: Acuity Scheduling appointment booking software interface
pros: Built-in payment processing via Stripe, Square, and PayPal on all plans
Per-account pricing (not per-user) makes it cost-effective for teams with multiple staff
HIPAA compliance available on higher-tier plans — great for healthcare providers
Deep client management: intake forms, packages, subscriptions, and invoicing built in
cons: No free plan — only a 7-day trial before you must pay
Steeper learning curve than Calendly; more options upfront can feel overwhelming
Fewer third-party integrations (~37) compared to Calendly's 100+
item_a.best_for: Service-based businesses like salons, coaches, and healthcare providers who need scheduling, payments, and client management in one place.

item_b.name: Calendly
pros: Generous free plan with unlimited bookings for one event type — no credit card required
100+ integrations including Zoom, Teams, Salesforce, HubSpot, and Slack
Extremely fast setup — a booking page can go live in under 10 minutes
Round-robin and collective scheduling make it powerful for sales and recruiting teams
cons: Per-user pricing gets expensive quickly for growing teams
Less capable for service businesses that need built-in invoicing or package selling
Branding customization is limited compared to Acuity
item_b.best_for: Professionals, sales teams, and recruiters who need frictionless virtual meeting scheduling with minimal setup.

comparison_summary: Calendly wins for individuals, remote teams, and anyone who needs a fast, clean meeting scheduler with a free entry point and deep integrations. Acuity Scheduling is the stronger pick for service businesses — spas, coaches, clinics — that need payments and client management baked in. They're solving slightly different problems, which is why the 'better' tool really depends on your business model.
comparison_intro: Acuity Scheduling vs Calendly is one of the most common debates in the scheduling software world, and for good reason — both tools are polished, popular, and genuinely useful. But should you choose Acuity Scheduling or Calendly? The answer changes dramatically depending on what you actually do for a living. Understanding the difference between Acuity Scheduling and Calendly comes down to one core question: are you scheduling meetings, or selling your time as a service? With Acuity Scheduling compared to Calendly, you'll find that which is better depends entirely on whether you need a lean meeting tool or a full client-booking engine.
verdict_text: Calendly edges out Acuity Scheduling for most users thanks to its free plan, faster setup, and broader integration ecosystem — but Acuity is the clear winner for service-based businesses that live and die by appointments.

key_differences.item_b: Free forever plan with unlimited bookings (1 event type)

key_differences.item_b: Per user — costs scale linearly with team size

key_differences.item_a: Full client records, intake forms, packages, subscriptions

seo_content:
Most people framing this as a head-to-head miss the most important insight: Calendly and Acuity Scheduling aren't really the same type of product. Calendly is a meeting scheduler. Acuity is an appointment booking platform. That distinction matters more than any feature checklist.

Calendly vs Acuity Scheduling ultimately splits into two camps based on how you make money. If you're in sales, recruiting, consulting, or any role where the goal is to get someone on a call, Calendly is purpose-built for you. It's fast, clean, and has an ecosystem of 100+ integrations that connect directly into the tools teams already use — Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Microsoft Teams. The free plan is genuinely usable, not just a teaser, and getting a booking link in front of someone takes minutes.

Acuity Scheduling compared to Calendly tells a different story when you're running a yoga studio, a therapy practice, a hair salon, or a personal training business. Those businesses don't just need someone to pick a time — they need payment collected upfront, intake forms filled out, packages tracked, and reminders sent. Acuity handles all of that natively, and its per-account pricing model means a salon with five stylists pays one flat rate instead of five separate seats.

From what I've seen in real-world use, Calendly wins on speed and simplicity. A new user can have a professional booking page live in under 10 minutes. Acuity's deeper feature set comes with a proportionally steeper learning curve — there are more menus, more settings, and more decisions to make before your first booking goes through. That complexity pays off for service businesses, but it's unnecessary friction for someone who just wants to eliminate back-and-forth emails.

On pricing, Acuity Scheduling or Calendly depends on team size. For a solo user, Calendly's free tier wins outright. For a team of five or more in a service business, Acuity's per-account model can actually be cheaper than Calendly's per-seat pricing. Do the math for your specific headcount before deciding.

One area where Acuity has a clear, undisputed edge: healthcare. HIPAA compliance is available on Acuity's Powerhouse plan — Calendly simply doesn't offer it. For any medical or wellness practice handling protected health information, that's not a nice-to-have, it's a requirement.

The Acuity Scheduling vs Calendly debate doesn't have a universal winner. I'd pick Calendly for any knowledge worker or remote team, and Acuity for any business where the appointment itself is the product being sold. Know which category you're in, and the choice becomes obvious.

faq.q: Is Acuity Scheduling better than Calendly?
faq.a: Neither is objectively better — they serve different use cases. Acuity Scheduling is better for service-based businesses (salons, coaches, clinics) that need built-in payments, client records, and HIPAA compliance. Calendly is better for professionals and teams who need fast, frictionless meeting scheduling with a wide integration ecosystem and a free entry point.

faq.q: Should I choose Acuity Scheduling or Calendly?
faq.a: Choose Calendly if you primarily schedule virtual meetings, work in sales or recruiting, or want a free plan with zero commitment. Choose Acuity Scheduling if you run a service business where clients pay for appointments, need to manage multiple staff members under one account, or require HIPAA compliance for healthcare-related bookings.

faq.q: What is the difference between Acuity Scheduling and Calendly?
faq.a: The four biggest differences are: (1) Calendly offers a free forever plan; Acuity only offers a 7-day trial. (2) Acuity uses per-account pricing, making it more affordable for multi-staff businesses; Calendly charges per user. (3) Acuity has native payment processing, client packages, and intake forms on all plans; Calendly is more focused on meeting scheduling. (4) Calendly has 100+ integrations versus Acuity's ~37, making it better suited for complex tech stacks.

faq.q: Does Acuity Scheduling have a free plan?
faq.a: No. As of 2026, Acuity Scheduling does not offer a free-forever plan. New users get a 7-day free trial that includes access to all features, but a paid subscription starting at $20/month (monthly billing) is required after that.

Yes, but only on higher-tier plans. HIPAA compliance is available on Acuity's Powerhouse and Enterprise plans, making it a viable option for healthcare providers and therapists who handle protected health information. Calendly does not offer HIPAA compliance.

meta_description: Acuity Scheduling vs Calendly: a detailed 2026 breakdown of pricing, features, and use cases. Wondering whether to use Acuity Scheduling or Calendly? We compare integrations, free plans, payment tools, and more so you can choose the right scheduler.
